data.title: Refactor purge-non-project-tickets.sh to use ticket purge-bridge
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor `purge-non-project-tickets.sh` to call `ticket purge-bridge` instead of accessing `.tickets-tracker/` directly. Preserve existing flag surface and behavior. Add to boundary hook allowlist as a thin wrapper.

**Why**: Moves bridge-ticket purge logic into the ticket CLI boundary. Eliminates a direct tracker accessor.

**Scope**:
- IN: purge-non-project-tickets.sh refactor to use `.claude/scripts/dso ticket purge-bridge` (via shim), preserve flag surface and behavior, existing test suite passes, boundary-hook allowlist entry with ticket citation
- OUT: Changes to `ticket purge-bridge` itself (epic 4931-f639 scope); any Jira API interaction (purge is local-only)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, purge-non-project-tickets.sh invokes `.claude/scripts/dso ticket purge-bridge` and preserves the existing flag surface
  ← Satisfies: "purge-non-project-tickets.sh calls ticket purge-bridge"
- When this story is complete, purge-non-project-tickets.sh's existing test suite passes with no behavioral regression
  ← Satisfies: "Each refactored script passes its existing test suite with no behavioral regression"
- When this story is complete, purge-non-project-tickets.sh is added to the boundary hook allowlist with an inline comment citing epic e1b2-2653
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] purge-bridge is LOCAL-ONLY (no Jira API calls) — this refactor must not introduce any network-dependent behavior
- [Dependency] Depends on `ticket purge-bridge` from epic 4931-f639 (inter-epic)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
